mnemonics: Twisted Pairs Are Fun: Twisted Pair, Coaxial, Fiber-Optic (TPCF) - Think 'Twisted Pairs Can Fly'
Fiber's Fast Family: Single-mode (Speed, Long Distance), Multi-mode (Many paths, Short distance)
keyPoints: Twisted Pair Cable: Two insulated copper wires twisted together to reduce electromagnetic interference (EMI). Common in Ethernet LANs.
Coaxial Cable: A single copper conductor surrounded by insulation, a braided metal shield, and an outer jacket. Offers higher bandwidth than twisted pair.
Fiber-Optic Cable: Uses pulses of light through glass/plastic fibers. Offers extremely high bandwidth, immunity to EMI, and long-distance transmission.
commonMistakes: Assuming all copper cables are the same (e.g., confusing Cat5e, Cat6, and Cat7 twisted pair capabilities).
Thinking single-mode fiber is always 'better'; it's optimized for long distances, while multi-mode is more cost-effective for short runs.
